latestRecords.title: Interrupted live run dirtied the workspace without matching changed-file artifacts
latestRecords.description: The live runner changed `docs/demos/README.md`, but the interrupted run artifacts still showed no changed files and no runner result bundle, leaving the persisted evidence out of sync with the observed workspace state.
latestRecords.reproductionNotes: Start the demo walkthrough dogfood run with the live runner, interrupt it before completion, then compare the working tree diff with the run directory contents.
latestRecords.suspectedCause: Changed-file capture relies on post-run persistence that never finished after the interrupted runner stage.

latestRecords.title: Live runner provided no meaningful heartbeat while work was in progress
latestRecords.description: During live dogfooding runs, the CLI left the operator waiting at `runner_started` for minutes without useful command or phase feedback, making it hard to distinguish slow execution from a hang.
latestRecords.reproductionNotes: Run a live `codex-cli` task with `pnpm gdh run ... --runner codex-cli` and observe the gap between `runner.started` and the eventual final state.
latestRecords.suspectedCause: The live progress surface records too little intermediate runner information for operator-facing summaries.

latestRecords.title: Benchmark summary made operators open raw JSON to find the governed run id
latestRecords.description: The benchmark CLI and report surface exposed the benchmark run id but not the linked governed run id directly, so the operator had to inspect `benchmark.run.json` to follow the underlying evidence.
latestRecords.reproductionNotes: Run `pnpm gdh benchmark run fresh-tests-dashboard-loading-wait --ci-safe --json` and compare the top-level CLI summary with the underlying benchmark artifact.
latestRecords.suspectedCause: The benchmark operator summary emphasizes score and case counts but omits one of the most important drill-down identifiers.

latestRecords.title: Workflow edit was auto-allowed instead of pausing for approval
latestRecords.description: A guarded dogfooding run that targeted `.github/workflows/ci.yml` evaluated as `allow` and began editing the workflow even though the expected operator behavior was an approval stop.
latestRecords.reproductionNotes: Run `pnpm gdh run runs/fixtures/dogfood/ci-workflow-comment-cleanup.md --runner codex-cli --approval-mode fail --json` in this repo and inspect the persisted policy decision before the operator interrupts the run.
latestRecords.suspectedCause: Policy matching treated spec-mentioned validation commands as a stronger allow signal than the protected workflow path intent.

latestRecords.title: Interrupted live run was marked resumable from runner_completed without runner result evidence
latestRecords.description: After the operator interrupted a live docs run during `runner_started`, the persisted run state claimed the run could resume from `runner_completed` even though no `runner.result.json` existed and the runner stage had not completed cleanly.
latestRecords.reproductionNotes: Interrupt `pnpm gdh run runs/fixtures/dogfood/demo-walkthrough-clarity.md --runner codex-cli --approval-mode fail --json` mid-run, then inspect `gdh status` and the persisted `run.json`.
latestRecords.suspectedCause: Interrupted-stage reconciliation promotes the pending stage too optimistically instead of requiring a full runner completion bundle.

latestRecords.title: Good docs run failed verification because the runner summary said CI-safe
latestRecords.description: A docs-only run made the intended README fix and passed the configured verification commands, but the overall run still failed because claim verification rejected the phrase `CI-safe` in the runner summary.
latestRecords.reproductionNotes: Run `pnpm gdh run runs/fixtures/dogfood/readme-benchmark-tier-note.md --runner codex-cli --approval-mode fail --json` and compare `verification.result.json` with the recorded runner summary and claim checks.
latestRecords.suspectedCause: The unsupported-certainty matcher is too broad for benign evidence-qualified wording in runner narration.
